title: Packages
body:
|version|loader availability|note|
|--:|:-:|:--|
|1.21.1|Fabric and Neoforge|part of [*season 2*](https://notes.highlysuspect.agency/mods/season2.html)|
|1.20.1|Fabric and Forge|later versions are part of *season 2*|
|Older versions|Fabric, sometimes Forge|Unmaintained, *season 1*|

*On Fabric 1.20.1, users of Sodium may need to install Indium, or models will look weird*

*On Neoforge, enable the "NeoForge Light Pipeline" in `Mods` -> `NeoForge` -> `Client settings` -> `NeoForge Light Pipeline`*

---

*Packages* adds packages, which can hold 8 stacks of one item.

* Construct Packages out of any solid block.
* Insert and remove items by clicking the front face. No need to use an empty hand.
* Hold CTRL to completely fill or empty a Package with one click.
* Interact with Packages *in your inventory* too, just like a Bundle.
* Make a package "sticky" so it won't forget its contents when empty.

The catch: Packages *only* hold 8 stacks. There are no storage upgrades. The size is not configurable. But packages can *nest* inside each other, and even stack when they contain the same contents!

Other than their size, Packages behave like cheap shulker boxes. Measure with a comparator, crush with a piston, and dispense with a dispenser; shulker box loader and unloader designs work great on Packages.

![A double chest with several packages in it, containing common vanilla materials, with varying fill levels](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/10DZYVis/images/9bee6ff5e6721bd259b566ceadf10ba4080bdb74.png)

## Credits

Kat came up with the Package Crafter model, unascribed came up with the idea for bundle interactions.

## Support

See the [minisite](https://notes.highlysuspect.agency/mods/packages.html) for more documentation.

Questions? Comments? Just lonely? Join me on Discord: https://highlysuspect.agency/discord/
